description_long: This object is a hammer with a highly unusual design, featuring a head that closely resembles an old Nokia mobile phone, complete with a keypad and display screen. The head is rectangular and slightly curved, with visible buttons and a screen area on one side, while the back is smooth and solid. The handle is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of a dark, matte material, likely plastic or metal, and is attached centrally to the base of the phone-shaped head. The overall construction suggests a novelty or conceptual tool rather than a standard hammer, but it retains the basic functional form of a hammer. The color scheme is predominantly dark blue and black, with lighter gray accents on the head. There are no visible signs of wear, branding, or additional attachments.
description: A novelty hammer with a head shaped like a classic Nokia phone and a long, dark cylindrical handle. The design is unique, blending tool and technology aesthetics.
description_view_0: Front view shows the phone-shaped hammer head with keypad and screen details, and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thickness of the hammer head and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the smooth, featureless back of the hammer head and the handle's attachment point.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the uniform thickness and profile of the head and handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely plastic or metal, with a smooth matte finish and no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular, phone-shaped head resembling a classic Nokia mobile phone, with a flat striking face and no claws or peen.
